headline: MARKET HOLIDAY — NYSE/Nasdaq closed Friday July 3 for observed Independence Day (July 4 falls on Saturday); Thursday July 2 close: Dow +1.14% to 52,900.07 (fresh record), S&P 500 flat, Nasdaq -0.82% as chip cohort sold off for a second session on BofA bubble note + Meta Compute overhang
summary: US equity markets are closed Friday July 3, 2026 for the federally observed Independence Day holiday (statutory July 4 falls on Saturday). Bond market closes at 2 PM ET. Thursday July 2 recap: Dow Jones Industrial Average closed +1.14% at 52,900.07 (all-time high), driven by defensive/traditional-economy names (Apple +5%, Visa/Walmart +2% each). S&P 500 essentially flat. Nasdaq Composite -0.82% as chipmakers continued the pullback that began Wednesday on BofA's bubble-risk indicator note — MU/INTC/AMD/TSM all extended losses. Soft June NFP (+57K vs +113K consensus) reinforced the September rate-cut narrative and supported bond-proxy utilities. Read-through into the long weekend: (1) DEFENSIVE ROTATION intact — money staying in Dow-heavy names, not returning to Nasdaq-100 leadership; (2) semi cohort now stares at a 3-day cooling window with no fresh catalyst until Monday; (3) TSMC June revenue expected around July 10 will be the first supply-side data point on whether the AI demand pull-through remains at May's 30% YoY pace; (4) two policy overhangs into next week — OpenAI 5% government-stake proposal (see paired entry) and the Section 232 semiconductor market report Commerce owed Trump on July 1 that could modify the January 25% tariff.

headline: OPENAI PROPOSES 5% US-GOVERNMENT EQUITY STAKE — Altman pitches Trump/Lutnick/Bessent an Alaska-Permanent-Fund-style vehicle where each leading US AI lab (potentially incl. Anthropic, GOOGL, META and even NVDA/MU/AMD) contributes 5% equity; would be worth ~$42.6B on OpenAI's recent $852B mark; FT/Bloomberg reports July 2
summary: FT reported Wednesday-into-Thursday July 2 (confirmed by Bloomberg and CNBC same evening) that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has begun 'conceptual' discussions with the Trump administration to grant the US government a 5% equity stake in OpenAI, worth roughly $42.6B at its most recent $852B valuation. Altman's broader proposal — reportedly raised with President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, plus outreach to Sen. Bernie Sanders — is that ALL leading US AI developers contribute the same 5% share into a sovereign-wealth vehicle modeled on the Alaska Permanent Fund, which currently holds ~$91B and pays annual dividends to Alaska residents. Potential participants named in reporting: Anthropic, Google, Meta, plus semiconductor makers Nvidia, Micron and AMD. Precedent: US government already took a 10% Intel stake earlier in the Trump term. Sanders has previously argued for 50%. Timing: FT characterized talks as early-stage; implementation would likely require congressional action. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MATERIAL POLICY OVERHANG for GOOGL/META/NVDA/MU/AMD — any 5% dilution is real, but the offset is a de-facto regulatory settlement that could unwind AI antitrust and export-control friction; (2) NEUTRAL-TO-POSITIVE for pure-play datacenter/power picks (CEG/VST/GEV/ETN/VRT) — they're not in the equity-stake conversation but benefit from any implicit government blessing of continued hyperscaler capex; (3) COMPETITIVE POSITIVE for PLTR — sits outside the AI-lab cohort and its US-government revenue mix looks less exposed to dilution; (4) sets up Monday July 6 volatility as market digests the proposal alongside the Section 232 semiconductor report that Commerce owes Trump.

headline: OKLO — DOE approves Documented Safety Analysis for Groves Isotope Test Reactor July 1; project moves from documentation into final pre-startup review (readiness review → startup approval → fuel load → criticality); first advanced reactor DSA on privately-owned commercial land; July 4 criticality target still live
summary: Oklo/Atomic Alchemy disclosed Wednesday July 1 that the US Department of Energy has approved the Documented Safety Analysis (DSA) for the Groves Isotope Test Reactor near Lockhart, Texas, under DOE's Reactor Pilot Program. Both the Preliminary Documented Safety Analysis (PDSA) and the DSA are now cleared, moving Groves from the documentation phase into DOE's final pre-startup review. Groves is a 15-MWt pool-type, water-cooled, non-pressurized reactor burning low-enriched uranium — notable as the FIRST advanced reactor to receive DSA approval on privately-owned land with fully commercial sourcing. Remaining path to criticality: (1) DOE readiness review; (2) DOE startup approval; (3) fuel loading; (4) startup testing; (5) first self-sustaining chain reaction. Company still guiding to first criticality by July 4 to align with DOE-organized US 250th-anniversary milestone push, though the July 1 DSA release did not commit a specific date. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MATERIAL POSITIVE FOR OKLO — the DSA approval is the single biggest regulatory de-risking event since NRC design criteria approval in May 2026 and directly undercuts the primary short thesis that Oklo cannot execute against public timelines; (2) POSITIVE FOR THE SMR/MICROREACTOR COMPLEX (SMR, X-energy) by demonstrating DOE's pilot-program pathway can move at commercial speed; (3) monitor over the long weekend for either a Friday-evening criticality release or a slippage disclosure — either headline hits before Monday's open.

headline: SECTION 232 SEMI REPORT DUE — Commerce Sec. Lutnick required to deliver Trump an update on the US-datacenter semiconductor market by July 1; report will inform whether the January 25% AI-chip tariff is modified, expanded, or paired with new licensing rules; decision window opens post-holiday
summary: Under the January 14, 2026 Proclamation 11002 that imposed the current 25% Section 232 tariff on a narrow set of advanced AI semiconductors, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick was required to deliver President Trump an updated market study on the datacenter semiconductor supply chain by July 1, 2026. That deadline has now passed; no public release yet as of Friday's holiday. The report gives Trump statutory authority to (a) leave the 25% tariff untouched, (b) raise or expand it to cover additional product codes, (c) pair it with new licensing conditions on China-destined shipments, or (d) create supply-chain-buildout carveouts that route around it. Backdrop: BIS January 2026 rule already shifted advanced-AI-chip exports to China/Macau to case-by-case review, and NVDA's China market share has fallen from >90% to ~50%. Read-through to House coverage: (1) POLICY BINARY EVENT — any tariff broadening lands squarely on NVDA/AMD/INTC/MU import structures and adds to Q3 gross-margin risk; (2) CARVEOUT SCENARIO IS POSITIVE for the fab-buildout complex — INTC (Arizona/Ohio), TSM (Arizona/Kaohsiung), AMAT/LRCX/KLAC and 4063.T (Shin-Etsu) all benefit from anything that further incentivizes US-domestic manufacturing; (3) sits alongside the OpenAI 5%-stake proposal as a two-part policy overhang defining the July setup for the semis cohort.

headline: NEOCLOUD DAY-2 — CRWV/Nebius/IREN extended Wednesday's selloff into Thursday's session; Seeking Alpha and Invezz both published contra-notes calling the reaction overdone given contractual lockups through 2032; CRWV volume 3x average two sessions running signals institutional repositioning, not retail panic
summary: Second-session damage in the neocloud cohort as the Meta Compute overhang persisted into Thursday July 2. CoreWeave, Nebius Group and IREN all traded lower again after Wednesday's rout (CRWV -13.92%, NBIS -17.01%, SMCI -5.73% on 3x-average volumes). Contra-side commentary emerged Thursday: Seeking Alpha argued 'this selloff makes no sense' given the $35B CRWV Meta backlog is contractually locked through 2032 and Meta cannot spin up hyperscale colocation capacity to replace it in the 2027-2029 window; Invezz made a similar case for Nebius' $27B Meta contract. Both stress that near-term revenue is safe and the concern is renewal/expansion optionality beyond 2032. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRUCTURAL RE-PRICING intact but valuation gap has opened — CRWV forward-multiple compression may draw in value buyers if Monday shows any stabilization; (2) POSITIVE FOR NVDA — every incremental hyperscaler is more silicon demand, not less; (3) POSITIVE FOR VRT/ETN/GEV — hyperscaler-scale power/cooling orders don't care whether the compute is monetized inside or outside Meta; (4) NEGATIVE FOR DLR/EQIX secondarily — Meta's build-out could bypass wholesale colocation capacity long-term.

headline: KOREA MEMORY MEGA-CAPEX — Samsung + SK Hynix + Seoul commit 800T KRW (~$518B) to build 4 new memory fabs in the southwest plus 81T KRW HBM packaging hub in Chungcheong; President Lee Jae-myung receives deep bows from Chairman Lee Jae-yong and Chairman Chey Tae-won at presidential briefing
summary: South Korean government and industry announced a joint 800-trillion-won (~$518B) commitment across four new memory-fab sites in the country's southwest, layered with an additional 81T KRW (~$52B) for an HBM (high-bandwidth memory) packaging hub in the Chungcheong region. The announcement was made at a presidential briefing attended by Samsung Chairman Lee Jae-yong and SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won, both of whom bowed deeply to President Lee Jae-myung after committing to the plan. Samsung's separate 2026 capex was already ramped to $73B (+22% YoY) to expand memory and advanced logic. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for MU relative to Korean memory competitors long-term but NEGATIVE optionality risk if aggressive Korean HBM4/HBM4E ramp compresses the pricing that Micron's Q4 $50B ± $1B guide assumes; (2) POSITIVE for 005930.KS and 000660.KS on the equity-story front — government backing removes financing overhang; (3) MATERIAL POSITIVE for the fab-equipment complex (ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, 8035.T, 4063.T, ENTG, AEHR) which will absorb a disproportionate share of the $518B; (4) NEGATIVE for TSM sub-2nm timeline — analysts warn racing for the same equipment could push out TSMC/Intel's advanced-node schedules.

headline: TSMC JUNE REVENUE ON DECK — May print was NT$416.98B (+30.1% YoY, +1.5% MoM, single-month record); June monthly release scheduled around July 10 will set the tone into Q3 as the first supply-side read on whether AI-chip pull-through remains at May's pace; sell-side has been calibrating models to a June deceleration
summary: Reminder: TSMC's June 2026 monthly revenue is expected around July 10 (TSMC typically publishes on the 10th trading day of the following month). May print was NT$416.98B (+30.1% YoY, +1.5% MoM, a single-month record) — the June number is the market's first supply-side confirmation of whether AI-chip demand is holding at May's pace or beginning to normalize as BofA's July 1 bubble-risk note implicitly warned. Read-through to House coverage: (1) BINARY OVERNIGHT EVENT for TSM/NVDA/AMD/AVGO/MU/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC when the print lands — a +30%-plus YoY sustain would refute the BofA bubble frame; anything below +20% would validate it; (2) also worth watching alongside the ASML Q2 earnings print (scheduled mid-July) which reports order backlog and gives a longer-cycle supply-side signal.
